mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending expectations, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in event studies.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in >58% of observations, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates above a 0.4% monthly threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative repricing of equities within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with rising aggregate wages and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth is followed by negative 60-day returns in >55% of periods, or the correlation coefficient between payroll growth and quarterly returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall economic output, signaling impending corporate earnings revisions and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in >62% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals a manufacturing recession that reduces capital expenditure and equity valuations over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 60-day returns in >57% of instances, or the signal fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio in a long-short portfolio over rolling 5-year windows.
